business.name: The Hoot Nannie
business.tagline: The Farmer & The Pitmaster. Farm-raised steaks, whole hog BBQ, and a full bourbon bar on Main Street.
business.subtitle: A Five Spur Steakhouse
sisterRestaurants: Johnny Ray's Smokehouse (formerly The Honey Hog) - Fallston, NC
Nannie's Fish Camp & Chicken Champ - Fallston, NC
Five Spur Steakhouse - Cherryville, NC (newest location)

farmOperation.name: Honey Hog Farm
farmOperation.model: Farm-to-table: the restaurant group raises much of their own meat

voice.tone: casual, rowdy, Southern, unpretentious, fun-loving, bold
voice.ownerPersona: Johnny Ray advertises wings and specials heavily on Facebook. The branding leans into the nicknames (Ole Johnny Ray, Neighbor Steve). The name 'Hoot Nannie' itself signals fun, rowdy, good-time energy.

brand.colorDirection: Faded brick building exterior on Main Street corner. Interior has retro country decor, bar area with warm lighting. Think aged wood, exposed brick, warm ambers, deep reds, charcoal smoke tones. Western/ranch influence.
brand.mood: Rowdy Southern roadhouse meets farm-to-table authenticity. Not polished, not trying to be fancy. Genuine, bold, smoky.
brand.archetype: The Outlaw Pitmaster - rebellious names (Dirty Sanchez burger), bold flavors, but backed by real farming and craft

fonts.display: CHECK Facebook/Instagram for actual logo typography. Likely a bold, rustic/western display face.

content.heroSubtext: Ole Johnny Ray and Neighbor Steve brought together the best of The Honey Hog and Nannie's Fish & Chicken, added steaks from local farms and 68 kinds of bourbon. Welcome to The Hoot Nannie.
content.originStory: Johnny Ray Bousselot started making tomato-and-bourbon barbecue sauces around 2012 out of Mount Holly, NC, selling them to local shops and eventually landing on shelves at Food Lion and Ingles. In 2018, he partnered with Tyler Jones to form Honey Hog Restaurant Group, opening The Honey Hog in Fallston, NC as a true farm-and-table operation, raising their own hogs, Wagyu cattle, and chickens. When a spot opened on Main Street in Forest City after COVID, Johnny Ray and his business partner Stephen Soto (aka Neighbor Steve) combined the best of their two existing restaurants: The Honey Hog's BBQ and Nannie's Fish Camp & Chicken Champ's Southern comfort food. They added farm-raised steaks from Beam Farms in Rutherford County and built out a full liquor bar with 68 different bourbons. The Hoot Nannie opened in mid-June 2021.

appetizers: Legendary Fried Cheese Curds - fresh curds from Brown Creek Creamery, hand-breaded and fried to order, served with house-made ranch and marinara (sells ~150 lbs per week)
burgers: Dirty Sanchez Burger - pulled pork BBQ, onion rings, handmade beef patty
sandwiches: Big Samantha Sandwich - club with red onion, lettuce, tomato, cheese, pickles on Texas Toast
Smokehouse Cuban - smoked ham, Swiss, mustard, homemade pork loin, dill pickles, pressed bun
Philly Cheese Steak with house chips
steaks: Farm-raised steaks from Beam Farms, Rutherford County
wings: Smoked Wings - smoked first, then fried. Multiple sauce options.
sides: House salad / Wedge Salad / Caesar Salad
bar: Signature cocktails (Tequila Sunrise, Irish Cream drinks)

content.menuFormat: Two-sided laminated menu: one side pricey, one side budget-friendly

testimonials.quote: If comfort food and good vibes ever had a rowdy little love child, its name would be Hoot Nannie.
The Cheese Curds may have been the best I've ever had. The smashed Double Bacon Cheeseburger was amazing.
The steak was delicious, cooked exactly as we wanted. Pet friendly too. Next time we get a steak craving we will for sure go back.
These were some of the best ribs I've ever had! Tender and juicy with a nice dark bark.
Smoked wings were unbelievable. Very good BBQ and the wings are the best in town.
Arriving in the parking lot drew me in because you can smell the grill. A great place to get a steak. Quite a cowboy's dream!
I ordered a Smokehouse Cuban with their homemade chips, absolutely amazing! The best kettle type chips I've eaten!
Best. Chicken. Sandwich. EVER. We will be back for sure! Crowded so go early!
This is a farm to table type of place, so most likely you will get food sourced locally as well as the beers they serve.
The classic crispy sandwich is so big and juicy! The drinks are really good too! So many choices on the cocktail menu!

sellingPoints: Farm-to-table: meat from their own Honey Hog Farm (126 acres owned, 140 leased) plus Beam Farms in Rutherford County
68 different bourbons, largest whiskey selection in western NC
Legendary fried cheese curds from Brown Creek Creamery (150 lbs/week)
Smoked-then-fried wings with house sauces, $0.50 wing Thursdays
Whole hog BBQ from a real pitmaster who started with sauce-making
Full liquor bar with signature cocktails
Downtown Main Street location in Forest City
Johnny Ray's signature sauces (sold in Food Lion and Ingles stores)
